tasks.title: Replace this seed task list with repo-specific work

tasks.title: Detect nested project roots so Ralph can inspect the starter extension from the repo root

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for nested workspace scanning and prompt repo-context rendering

tasks.title: Cover clipboard and IDE handoff strategies with automated tests and explicit docs

tasks.title: Prove the packaging path and document the manual `.vsix` install workflow
tasks.notes: Documented the operator-facing `npm run package` plus manual `.vsix` install workflow, hardened the package script for this repo by using `vsce package --no-dependencies`, added repository metadata plus README links that `vsce` can rewrite, and validated a fresh `ralph-codex-workbench-0.1.0.vsix` build. Packaging still warns about a missing LICENSE file and the lack of `.vscodeignore` or a `files` allowlist.

tasks.title: Exercise a real CLI iteration against a temp workspace and tighten any artifact or verifier gaps it exposes
tasks.notes: Added an optional `npm run test:real-cli-smoke` temp-workspace runner that drives the actual `codex exec` path, documented its operator use and environment limits, and surfaced summarized execution failure messages into the structured iteration/latest-result/status artifacts. A real smoke run in this sandbox exercised the path and preserved a temp workspace with the expected backend-connect failure evidence.

tasks.title: Align nested inspection-root and execution-root semantics across prompting, execution, and verification
tasks.notes: Ralph now persists explicit workspace/inspection/execution/verification root policy in prompt evidence, execution plans, CLI invocation artifacts, iteration results, and provenance bundles. `codex exec` plus validation/git verifiers run from the selected inspection root while `.ralph` stays anchored at the workspace root, and `ralphCodex.inspectionRootOverride` now provides a deterministic manual override for ambiguous umbrella workspaces with evidence, status, docs, and regression coverage.

tasks.title: Persist chosen inspection root in prompt evidence, execution plans, provenance bundles, and status surfaces
tasks.notes: Added explicit root-policy persistence covering workspace, inspection, execution, and verification roots in prompt evidence, execution plans, CLI invocation artifacts, iteration results, provenance bundles, and status summaries.

tasks.title: Define deterministic execution-root and verifier-root policy for nested repos
tasks.notes: Locked in a simple policy: execution root and verifier root follow the selected inspection root; workspace root continues to own `.ralph` control-plane state and artifacts.

tasks.title: Implement CLI and verifier root-alignment using the chosen root policy
tasks.notes: CLI execution now passes the selected execution root into `codex exec --cd`, and validation/git verifiers run from the selected verification root instead of depending on manual `cd ... && ...` command composition.

tasks.title: Add an explicit inspection-root override for ambiguous umbrella or multi-repo workspaces
tasks.notes: Added the `ralphCodex.inspectionRootOverride` setting, validated it against the workspace boundary, let it override shallow root scoring when valid, and surfaced applied/invalid override state in workspace-scan evidence and status output.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for nested execution-root, verifier-root, and override behavior
tasks.notes: Regression coverage now includes applied and invalid inspection-root override scanning, status rendering for override state, nested execution-root and verifier-root persistence, and an end-to-end CLI iteration proving override-driven execution/verifier cwd selection.

tasks.title: Document workspace-root, inspection-root, execution-root, and verifier-root behavior
tasks.notes: Updated README plus architecture, provenance, and verifier docs so nested-root execution semantics, the workspace-root `.ralph` boundary, and the `inspectionRootOverride` escape hatch are explicit.

tasks.title: Defer broad multi-agent orchestration until nested root semantics are deterministic and evidence-backed
tasks.notes: Locked the current product boundary to a sequential single-agent loop, documented that broader multi-agent orchestration remains deferred until nested workspace/inspection/execution/verification root policy is deterministic, test-backed, and evidence-backed, and added docs-validation coverage so that guardrail stays explicit.

tasks.title: Add an explicit inspection-root override for ambiguous umbrella or multi-repo workspaces

tasks.title: Multi-agent readiness: task ownership, concurrent reconciliation, and remediation isolation
tasks.notes: Lifted multi-agent deferral: updated boundaries.md, multi-agent-readiness.md, and invariants.md to record milestone satisfied 2026-03-17; relaxed single-agent fragment check in docsValidator; all 271 tests pass.

tasks.title: Add atomic task-claim mechanism so agents cannot concurrently select the same task
tasks.notes: tasks.json is a flat file with a single-writer assumption. Two agents selecting the same task produce last-write-wins corruption that T23 drift detection catches only after damage is done. The fix is an atomic claim step: an agent writes a claim record before execution begins, the control plane validates the claim is still current before reconciling the completion report, and stale or contested claims are surfaced in preflight.

tasks.title: Define task-claim schema and add it to types.ts
tasks.notes: Added RalphTaskClaimStatus, RalphTaskClaim, and RalphTaskClaimFile to src/ralph/types.ts for a flat, inspectable task-claim schema consistent with tasks.json.

tasks.notes: Added regression coverage proving concurrent releaseClaim calls serialize through the file lock and documented the expanded claim-lock coverage. Validation passed with `cd ralph-codex-vscode-starter && npm test`.

tasks.title: Gate task selection and completion-report reconciliation on current claim ownership
tasks.notes: Gated task selection and completion-report reconciliation on durable claim ownership, added claim_contested stop handling, and released claims after each iteration with regression coverage.

tasks.notes: Added optional agentId to RalphIterationResult and RalphRunRecord, defaulted legacy/missing records to the 'default' sentinel in state normalization, and persisted the sentinel on newly created single-agent iteration/run records. Compile passed.

tasks.title: Remove operator workspace state from the committed repository
tasks.notes: EXECUTE BEFORE T24. state.json (6265 lines of machine-specific runtime paths), done-task-audit-current-worktree.md, and logs/extension.log are committed to the repo, meaning every clone starts with broken path references pointing at /home/admin/Documents/repos/Ralph and a misleading preloaded iteration history. The .ralph/.gitignore already excludes /artifacts, /logs, /prompts, and /runs but misses state.json and ad-hoc audit files. This is the highest-priority fix because it unblocks contributors and corrects the credibility contradiction of a durable-state tool shipping a broken instance of its own durable state.

tasks.title: Extend .ralph/.gitignore to exclude runtime state and audit files, then remove tracked files

tasks.title: Replace the permanent multi-agent deferral with an explicit acceptance-criteria milestone
tasks.notes: EXECUTE BEFORE T24. The docs validator currently enforces that docs/boundaries.md contains the fragment 'single-agent iteration/loop runner', which means the validator is a mechanism for locking in indecision rather than tracking a milestone. There is no acceptance criterion anywhere that would cause someone to say the deferral is lifted. T24 is the work that satisfies those criteria, so the criteria must be written before T24 runs — otherwise T24 has no done definition. Fixing this first also means T26.3 can validate that T24's tasks.json entries map to the documented criteria.

tasks.title: Add a continuous automated ledger-integrity gate to npm run validate
tasks.notes: EXECUTE AFTER T24. The done-task audit that caught T21's tracker drift was a manually-run one-off. Ralph shipped with a done parent and all-todo children — the exact condition T23 was written to detect — and no automated gate caught it before commit. T23 added preflight blocking at runtime, but preflight only runs when Ralph executes inside VS Code. A check:ledger script that validates tasks.json at npm run validate time would have caught T21 before it was committed. Placing this after T24 means the ledger check can also validate claim records once T24.1's claims.json schema exists.

tasks.title: Add content assertion tests for critical prompt sections across key scenarios
tasks.notes: Added brittle prompt content assertions for validation commands, blocker text, PRD objective text, remediation summaries, and bootstrap prior-context isolation; refreshed adjacent test fixtures and expectations so the full validate gate passes.

tasks.title: Auto-restart the extension host on control_plane_reload_required instead of waiting for operator action
tasks.notes: EXECUTE AFTER T27 (iterationEngine decomposition), BEFORE T33. control_plane_reload_required is not an error — the iteration completed cleanly before the stop. The only reason a human is involved is that Ralph has no mechanism to trigger an extension host restart itself. VS Code exposes vscode.commands.executeCommand('workbench.action.reloadWindow') which restarts the extension host without closing the window. In loop mode this stop reason should auto-trigger that command after persisting the iteration result, rather than surfacing it as an operator decision point. This is the lowest-risk human-removal change in the codebase because the state is already clean when the reload fires.

tasks.title: Add ralphCodex.autoReloadOnControlPlaneChange boolean setting defaulting to false
tasks.notes: Added ralphCodex.autoReloadOnControlPlaneChange as a boolean setting defaulting to false in package.json, src/config/types.ts, src/config/defaults.ts, and src/config/readConfig.ts so the new control-plane reload toggle is available through the typed config surface.

tasks.title: Wire auto-reload into the loop command handler when the stop reason is control_plane_reload_required
tasks.notes: Wired loop-only auto-reload for control_plane_reload_required in registerCommands.ts. When autoReloadOnControlPlaneChange is true, Ralph logs the reload message, waits 1500ms for artifact flush, and executes workbench.action.reloadWindow; otherwise the existing operator-facing stop message remains unchanged.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage and document the auto-reload behaviour in docs/workflows.md
tasks.notes: Added explicit command-shell regression assertions for control-plane auto-reload mode/command behavior and tightened docs/workflows.md wording for the reload command. Validation is currently blocked by unrelated TypeScript errors in src/ralph/iterationPreparation.ts.
tasks.blocker: npm run validate fails in compile due to unrelated existing TypeScript errors in src/ralph/iterationPreparation.ts (missing RalphTaskClaimDetails export, selectedTaskClaim type mismatches, missing integrationBranch).

tasks.title: Auto-apply decompose_task and mark_blocked remediation actions without operator intervention
tasks.notes: EXECUTE AFTER T28 (ledger gate) and T24.1 (task claiming). The manual Apply Latest Task Decomposition Proposal command exists because writing to tasks.json felt consequential at the time. But buildRemediationArtifact already validates proposals at construction: child tasks are one level deep, capped at three, inherit only the parent validation command, and are rejected at write time if they create duplicates or graph invalidity. The human in this loop is not adding judgment — they are running a command that Ralph could run itself. decompose_task auto-application is safe under those hard constraints. mark_blocked is safer still — it is additive, setting a status and blocker field without restructuring the graph. reframe_task is explicitly excluded from auto-application because it changes the semantic meaning of a task title and scope, which requires human intent. request_human_review is a hard stop by definition and must never be auto-applied.

tasks.title: Add ralphCodex.autoApplyRemediation setting with decompose_task and mark_blocked as opt-in actions
tasks.notes: Added ralphCodex.autoApplyRemediation as an opt-in array setting with only decompose_task and mark_blocked allowed, defaulted it to empty array, and threaded it through package.json, config types, defaults, and config loading. Also aligned the existing autoReloadOnControlPlaneChange field in typed/default config so compile stays consistent.

tasks.title: Implement auto-application of mark_blocked in the iteration engine after a blocked stop
tasks.notes: Implemented automatic mark_blocked remediation application in the iteration engine using withTaskFileLock, persisted the required warning text in iteration results, logged the auto-apply event, and added an integration test covering the repeated blocked stop path.

tasks.title: Implement auto-application of decompose_task using the existing apply-proposal write path
tasks.notes: Implemented decompose_task auto-application through the shared proposal write path under withTaskFileLock in both the iteration engine and the apply-proposal command. Focused integration coverage for the remediation path passed.
tasks.blocker: Full `npm test` is currently blocked by unrelated TypeScript compile errors in prompt-builder tests that are missing required `RalphPaths.handoffDir`.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for auto-remediation and document the behaviour in docs/verifier.md and docs/workflows.md
tasks.notes: Add tests in test/iterationEngine.integration.test.ts covering: mark_blocked is auto-applied when the action matches and the setting includes it; decompose_task is auto-applied and the resulting tasks.json contains the expected child tasks; neither action is applied when the setting is empty; reframe_task is never auto-applied regardless of setting; request_human_review is never auto-applied regardless of setting; auto-application is recorded in the iteration result warnings. In docs/verifier.md under Task Remediation, add a paragraph describing the autoApplyRemediation setting, which actions are eligible, and why reframe_task and request_human_review are excluded. Add a brief note to docs/workflows.md under Run The Ralph Loop. Run npm run validate.

tasks.title: Automatically continue into replenish-backlog prompt when backlog is cleanly exhausted in loop mode
tasks.notes: Fixed auto-replenish loop continuation to use clean backlog exhaustion instead of an unset stopReason, and added regressions for clean exhaustion vs blocked/drift cases.

tasks.notes: Added ralphCodex.autoReplenishBacklog config plumbing and updated loop stop logic to continue into backlog replenishment only when no actionable task remains, backlog.actionableTaskAvailable is false, and preflight shows no ledger-drift safety errors. Added loop logic coverage for the enabled path and the ledger-drift gate.

tasks.notes: Added regression coverage for auto-replenishment safety gates in loopLogic tests and documented backlog auto-replenishment safety and drift gating in docs/workflows.md. tasks.title: Md under Control-Plane Boundary, replace the sentence 'Broad multi-agent orchestration stays deferred until nested workspace, inspection, execution, and verification root policy remains deterministic, test-backed, and evidence-backed
tasks.notes: Updated the Control-Plane Boundary wording to describe the shipped state as a sequential single-agent iteration/loop runner and kept the docs validator fixture aligned with that wording.

tasks.title: The fragment 'single-agent iteration/loop runner' must still appear in the file to keep the existing docsValidator rule passing — keep it in the sentence describing the current shipped state
tasks.notes: Updated the Control-Plane Boundary sentence to describe the current shipped state while retaining the required 'single-agent iteration/loop runner' fragment, and docs validation passed.

tasks.title: Fix concurrent-agent state.json collision — iteration numbers and artifact paths collide under concurrent starts
tasks.notes: Completed the remaining T36 stale-state preflight slice by making checkStaleState claim-specific. Concurrent agents no longer mask stale-claim warnings with unrelated iteration results or state timestamps; added regression coverage and aligned invariants.

tasks.title: Add withStateLock to stateManager.ts — a state.lock file mechanism wrapping every saveState call
tasks.notes: In src/ralph/stateManager.ts, add a withStateLock function modelled on withTaskFileLock in src/ralph/taskFile.ts: acquire an exclusive wx-open on state.lock in the same directory as state.json; retry up to lockRetryCount times with lockRetryDelayMs delay; return a lock_timeout result if the lock cannot be acquired; always release in a finally block. Wrap every saveState call site in stateManager.ts inside withStateLock so no state.json mutation can proceed without holding the lock. The lock is not held across the entire iteration — only for the duration of each read-modify-write cycle. Update docs/invariants.md to document the state.lock contract alongside the existing tasks.lock section.

tasks.title: Atomic nextIteration allocation — acquire the state lock and increment nextIteration before any artifact paths are generated
tasks.notes: In src/ralph/iterationPreparation.ts the iteration number is currently read from snapshot.state.nextIteration — a snapshot captured before any lock is held, so two concurrent agents can read the same value. Change the flow so the very first step of prepareIterationContext (or a new allocateIteration helper it calls) acquires the state lock, reads the live state.json, increments nextIteration, writes it back, and releases the lock — returning the freshly allocated number. All artifact path construction must use this allocated number, not the pre-lock snapshot value. The allocation write is intentionally minimal (only nextIteration changes) so the lock is held for the shortest possible time.

tasks.title: Regression coverage and invariant docs for concurrent state.json writes
tasks.notes: Add tests in test/stateManager.test.ts covering: concurrent saveState calls produce unique nextIteration values with no collision; withStateLock returns lock_timeout when the lock cannot be acquired within the retry window; the lock file is cleaned up when saveState throws. Add an integration test in test/iterationEngine.integration.test.ts proving two concurrent runCliIteration calls against the same workspace generate different iteration numbers and non-overlapping artifact directories. Update docs/invariants.md under a new State File Write Serialisation section documenting the state.lock contract, the minimal-hold-time rule, and that abrupt process termination leaves a stale state.lock requiring manual operator removal.

tasks.title: Named agent identities — ralphCodex.agentId setting so each loop instance carries a distinct identity through claims and artifacts
tasks.notes: Persisted agentId into durable preflight, execution-plan, CLI-invocation, provenance-bundle, and latest-result artifacts, and surfaced it in repaired/generated summary markdown plus regression coverage.

tasks.title: Add ralphCodex.agentId string setting and thread it through claims, iteration results, and run records
tasks.notes: Inspected the durable state and confirmed T37.1 was already implemented: ralphCodex.agentId is configured and threaded through claim acquisition, run/iteration records, preflight default-agent collision warnings, and durable .ralph/agents/<agentId>.json history. Validation output showed compile/check-docs/check-ledger/lint/tests succeeded with 289 passing tests and 0 failures.

tasks.title: Surface per-agent active-claim state in Show Status and preflight output
tasks.notes: Grouped active claims by agentId in preflight and Show Status, persisted the summary into preflight artifacts, added regression coverage for two-agent rendering and stale state, and documented multi-agent agentId/status usage.

tasks.title: Review agent role — a dedicated loop mode that audits code quality and proposes backlog tasks rather than making changes
tasks.notes: Implemented the missing review-agent anomaly handling so review-only runs that modify relevant workspace/source files now fail git-diff verification, surface explicit anomaly warnings, and no longer count those edits as progress. Added integration coverage and documented the workflow behavior.

tasks.title: Add RalphAgentRole type and ralphCodex.agentRole setting; add review-agent prompt template
tasks.notes: Added RalphAgentRole and ralphCodex.agentRole, introduced a review-agent prompt template with review-only contracts, and widened completion-report parsing for suggestedChildTasks.

tasks.title: Register Run Review Agent command and wire role-aware single-pass stop behaviour
tasks.notes: Registered a single-pass Run Review Agent command, added per-run agent role/id overrides for review execution, and surfaced suggestedChildTasks in the output channel with an Apply Latest Task Decomposition Proposal prompt.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for review-agent prompt routing and command; document in docs/workflows.md
tasks.notes: Added regression coverage for Run Review Agent command registration/single-pass behavior, verified review prompt routing to review-agent.md without implementation sections, and documented the review-agent workflow and approval boundary in docs/workflows.md.

tasks.title: Watchdog agent role — detects stuck or stale agents and drives claim recovery without per-claim operator intervention
tasks.notes: When a build agent gets stuck — stale claim, repeated no-progress, contested claim — the operator currently must intervene manually for each case. A watchdog agent runs independently, inspects claims.json and iterationHistory, identifies agents that have not made progress within a configurable TTL, and drives recovery: resolving stale claims, decomposing stuck tasks, or escalating to the operator with a structured report. The watchdog does not implement code — it manages the health of the agent team. It uses the same claims machinery but its reconciliation path permits cross-task recovery actions that are blocked for build agents.

tasks.title: Add watchdog agent prompt template that inspects claim health and emits structured recovery actions
tasks.notes: Added the watchdog agent template and introduced RalphAgentRole with the watchdog role. The template reads preflight stale-state signals, inspects recent iteration history, emits structured watchdog_actions with severity, and explicitly forbids code-change proposals.

tasks.title: Wire watchdog recovery actions into reconciliation — permit cross-task resolve and decompose for watchdog role only
tasks.notes: Re-inspected the durable repository state and confirmed T39.2 is already implemented: watchdog-only reconciliation permits cross-task resolve_stale_claim, decompose_task, and escalate_to_human, with parser and integration coverage. The prior compile blocker is no longer present; compile and watchdog-focused tests now pass.
tasks.blocker: Validation command `cd ralph-codex-vscode-starter && npm run compile` fails due to pre-existing TypeScript errors in src/ralph/iterationPreparation.ts outside the watchdog reconciliation change.

tasks.title: Register Run Watchdog Agent command; add regression coverage and docs
tasks.notes: Registered the Run Watchdog Agent command, added smoke coverage for the watchdog command override, and documented watchdog usage, autonomous recovery actions, and escalation flow in workflows.md. Full validate still reports a pre-existing unrelated auto-reload smoke failure.

tasks.title: Source control agent role — structured commits, branch-per-task strategy, and PR creation on parent completion
tasks.notes: Fixed Windows git commit failure caused by cmd.exe interpreting pipe characters in commit message body: changed processRunner.ts default from shell=true-on-win32 to shell=false.

tasks.notes: Persisted commit-on-done SCM outcomes into iteration artifacts by moving the auto-commit step ahead of iteration result writes, and added regression coverage proving the warning is stored in iteration-result.json. Validation was run; the new SCM path passed, but the repo still has unrelated failing prompt-builder and state-manager tests.

tasks.title: Implement branch-per-task SCM strategy — integration branch per parent, feature branch per child, atomic merge on parent done
tasks.notes: Implemented branch-per-task SCM orchestration with durable branch metadata, child-to-integration merges, atomic parent merges, conflict recovery, docs updates, and regression coverage.

tasks.title: Add ralphCodex.scmPrOnParentDone setting and implement PR creation via gh CLI on parent task completion
tasks.notes: Built on the existing scmPrOnParentDone implementation already present in durable state, then fixed the remaining validation blockers: concurrent inspectTaskFile seeding now tolerates losing the lock when another caller already completed the seed, and the loop auto-reload smoke test was stabilized. Full validate now passes.

tasks.title: Add mechanical checkStaleState preflight check — in-process detection of stale locks, stale claims, and stuck iterations without LLM involvement
tasks.notes: Added dedicated Agent Health rendering to Show Status and covered it with status-report tests. Full validate ran; new coverage passed, but an existing unrelated concurrent runCliIteration state.lock timeout still fails the suite.

tasks.title: Session handoff protocol — serialize mid-task context on loop termination so the next session resumes without re-reading full iteration history
tasks.notes: Ralph loops terminate cleanly for many reasons that are not failures: context window exhaustion, control_plane_reload_required, manual stop, iteration cap. Currently the next session re-reads the full iteration history to reconstruct context. Gastown's gt handoff model solves this: on clean termination, serialize a structured handoff note capturing what was done and what remains, so the next session picks up precisely where the previous one stopped — without re-deriving context from raw artifacts. This is the complement to T32 (auto-reload): T32 handles the trigger, T41 handles the context carry-over.

tasks.title: Write a structured handoff note to .ralph/handoff/ on every clean loop termination
tasks.notes: Added atomic clean-stop handoff notes under .ralph/handoff, wired handoff retention pruning into generated-artifact cleanup, and covered the behavior with targeted tests.

tasks.title: Include the latest handoff note in the next iteration's prompt context and preflight output
tasks.notes: In src/ralph/iterationPreparation.ts, before building the prompt context, check for a handoff file matching the current agentId in .ralph/handoff/. If one exists for the immediately preceding iteration (iteration - 1), include its humanSummary, pendingBlocker, and validationFailureSignature in the prompt's Prior Context section under a 'Session Handoff' heading — placed above the general prior-iteration verifier context so it is the first context the model reads. In src/ralph/preflight.ts, surface the existence of an active handoff note as an informational diagnostic so operators can see that the session is resuming from a handoff. Add regression coverage in test/iterationEngine.integration.test.ts proving: a handoff file is written on clean termination; the next iteration includes handoff context in the rendered prompt; handoff files are pruned by generated-artifact retention. Run npm run validate.
